mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate above 5.0 percent signals deteriorating household income and consumer spending power, which forces equity valuations lower over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.03 over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and business confidence, driving strong equity market appreciation over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in retail sales and housing starts fail to predict positive 60-day returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production reflects robust manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which supports corporate earnings growth and generates positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Positive industrial production growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns during tightening periods, with Rank IC consistently below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index levels above 300 trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity multiples and produces negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally with positive 20-day returns in > 55% of instances when CPI exceeds 300, invalidating the tightening penalty mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with falling unemployment rates confirm a strengthening labor market, which boosts consumer spending forecasts and drives positive equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising payrolls and falling unemployment fails to predict positive 20-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.04 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth falling below zero confirms a technical recession, which destroys corporate profit expectations and forces sustained negative equity market performance.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns remain positive in > 50% of quarters where GDP growth turns negative, breaking the recession penalty link.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent stabilization in housing starts and retail sales indicates a bottoming consumer demand cycle, which reduces recession fears and triggers positive short-term equity rallies.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns do not significantly exceed baseline during periods of synchronized housing and retail stabilization (t-test p > 0.05).
